title: Question about the On Air version of episode 24
display_title: Question about the On Air version of episode 24
wikitext:
[]: Question about the On-Air version of episode 24             Quote Post by hirnkrieg » Wed Apr 16, 2014 6:53 pm At various times I've heard the rumor (used to underpin that EoE is the 'real' ending) that the On-Air version of episode 24 included animatics of scenes that were later used in End of Evangelion. I thought these scenes were only added in the Platinum Box-version. It seesms like the only way to confirm this would be by having watched the initial broadcast, since there doesn't seem to be a Rip of the On-Air version floating around.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Apr 16, 2014 8:20 pm You're correct. the on-air version of 24 had (very rough) previews of 25, not EoE. People can reasonably be confused by this, however, because the later releases did do some degree of retcon work there, and also because the original preview contains some shots that were in both 25 and 25'. There's also the complication that it seems 25' is based on a script that was originally meant to be 25, but was scrapped due to various production issues (though 26' is entirely original to EoE). Apparently this was changed at the last minute, which is why some of the very EoE-like shots made it to full production and got worked into the instrumentality narrative without much explanation.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 17, 2014 4:11 am You sure it was the On-Air and not the DC versions? I have the original ADV director's cut releases that include the original On-Air and I don't remember seeing the animatics in the preview for those. Though I think the DC versions had animatics, including some of Eva-02 fighting the MPE's.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 17, 2014 5:41 am My copy of the Perfect:Platinum has the “Air” preview in the Extended Edition, and storyboards for “A World That’s Ending (Do You Love Me?)” in the Original version. Wasn’t there a next-episode preview that was live-action? (I’m not referring to the live-action scenes from EoE, used or unused.) I could have sworn that I saw one where it was just the camera going through a door or something, with Misato’s narration over it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 17, 2014 7:44 am Ornette wrote: You sure it was the On-Air and not the DC versions?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Apr 17, 2014 7:49 am Run by me again why the overlaps are problematic?

[]:             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Thu Apr 17, 2014 1:31 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Wasn’t there a next-episode preview that was live-action? (I’m not referring to the live-action scenes from EoE, used or unused.) I could have sworn that I saw one where it was just the camera going through a door or something, with Misato’s narration over it.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 17, 2014 5:17 pm Monk Ed wrote: Run by me again why the overlaps are problematic?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Apr 17, 2014 9:15 pm Heaven Piercing Man wrote: That's from the Video version of EoE, which was presented as two separate episodes instead of one long movie. The preview you saw was for 26’, at the end of Air.

[]:             Quote Post by hirnkrieg » Fri Apr 18, 2014 2:46 pm So, to summarize the preview of OA 24 included only scenes that actually were in 25 (but some of those scenes overlap with 25'). And the Director's cut of 24 had animatics of Unit 02 fighting the MPE in its preview (strange that the DC should introduce scenes to the preview which don't end up being in the next episode). @NemZ: May I ask on which release you watched the OA versions? AFAIK, there is no release of the actual TV versions. We know this because the initial broadcast had furigana in the title cards , which isn't present in any of the releases.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 18, 2014 3:36 pm hirnkrieg wrote: @NemZ: May I ask on which release you watched the OA versions?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 18, 2014 4:17 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I’m wondering where I could have possibly seen that

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Apr 18, 2014 6:45 pm That makes the most sense, though "back in the old days" for me would be around 2009 or 2010. I hadn't really watched Eva before then. (I was still going through Nadia for the first time after deciding that I actually anime with films like AKIRA.)
summary: []: Question about the On-Air version of episode 24             Quote Post by hirnkrieg » Wed Apr 16, 2014 6:53 pm At various times I've heard the rumor (used to underpin that EoE is the 'real' ending) that the On-Air version of episode 24 included animatics of scenes that were later used in End of Evangelion. I thought these scenes were only added in the Platinum Box-version. It seesms like the only way to confirm this
